results.question: Can you tell me show the full subject outline for ARE6133 Analog Electronics.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARE6133 Title: Analog Electronics Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce the design and analysis of analog circuits using operational amplifiers. Assessment: - Lab: 10% - Assignment: 15% - Test/Quiz: 15% - Final Examination: 60% Laboratory: - Operational Amplifier Circuits and Their Typical Applications - Voltage Regulators Course Contents: - Operational amplifier fundamentals - Active filters - Practical op-amp limitations - Op-amp voltage regulators - Oscillators - Nonlinear circuit applications Course Learning Outcomes: - Analyse the ideal and practical op-amp circuits. - Analyse the voltage regulator and oscillator circuits. - Analyse the active filters and non-linear circuits. - Demonstrate the op-amp and voltage regulator circuits. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Analog Electronics Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce the design and analysis of analog circuits using operational amplifiers. Assessment: - Lab: 10% - Assignment: 15% - Test/Quiz: 15% - Final Examination: 60% Laboratory: - Operational Amplifier Circuits and Their Typical Applications - Voltage Regulators Course Contents: - Operational amplifier fundamentals - Active filters - Practical op-amp limitations - Op-amp voltage regulators - Oscillators - Nonlinear circuit applications Course Learning Outcomes: - Analyse the ideal and practical op-amp circuits. - Analyse the voltage regulator and oscillator circuits. - Analyse the active filters and non-linear circuits. - Demonstrate the op-amp and voltage regulator circuits. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

results.question: Show the full subject outline for MMD6123 Digital Fabrication and Prototyping.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: MMD6123 Title: Digital Fabrication and Prototyping Aliases: MID6113, Rapid Modelling Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 56 hours Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To build individual skills in producing physical 3D concepts using traditional and modern prototyping tools both manual or by machine for visualization, evaluation of design and conveying of ideations and concepts in the stages of the interface design process. Assessment: - Project 1: 20% - Project 2: 30% - Quiz: 10% - Presentation: 40% Course Contents: - Introduction to rapid modelling - Quick modelling for ideation process - Modelling for final mock up - Rapid prototyping Course Learning Outcomes: - Interpret the fundamentals of modelling and model making techniques used in industry. - Explain the principles of 3D printing and prototyping in the design industry. - Build physical models by using various techniques, approaches and mediums such as foam and 3D printing to industry standard. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Mapped by user clarification: same subject as MID6113 Rapid Modelling.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARR6133 Actuators and Sensors.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARR6133 Title: Actuators and Sensors Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 46 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To acquire technical knowledge in the field of actuators and sensors, and to design programmable logic controllers used in various industrial applications. Assessment: - Test: 10% - Assignment: 30% - Lab Assessment: 20% - Final exam: 40% Course Contents: - Basic principle of actuators and sensors - Actuators - Sensors - Gears - Controllers Course Learning Outcomes: - State the general operating principles of actuator, sensor and gear, and their technical properties in a basic drive system. - Describe the operation of actuators in industrial applications. - Demonstrate the usage of sensors in industrial applications. - Construct programmable logic controllers with sensor and actuator using practical software tools. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Actuators and Sensors Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 46 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To acquire technical knowledge in the field of actuators and sensors, and to design programmable logic controllers used in various industrial applications. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARE6143 Electronics Instrumentation.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARE6143 Title: Electronics Instrumentation Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 42 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide introduction to electronics instrumentation and measurements techniques. Assessment: - Assignment: 30% - Lab Assessment: 30% - Final exam: 40% Course Contents: - Introduction to basic lab equipment - DC power supply - Digital function generator - Digital multimeter - Frequency counter - Digital storage scope - Spectrum analyzer and vector signal analyzer Course Learning Outcomes: - Describe the operation of lab equipment including DC power supply, function generator, multimeter, frequency counter, digital storage scopes, and spectrum/signal analyzer. - Perform measurements using lab equipment including DC power supply, function generator, multimeter, and frequency counter. - Measure signals in the time-domain using digital storage scopes. - Measure signals in the frequency domain using spectrum analyzers/signal analyzers. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Electronics Instrumentation Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 42 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide introduction to electronics instrumentation and measurements techniques. results.question: What subjects are in Year 2?
results.expected_answer: Year 2 lists: Linear Systems & Signals; Electromagnetics with Applications; Electrical Machines and Power Systems; Robotics - Machine Design and Mechanisms; Introduction to Artificial Intelligence; Actuators and Sensors; Electronics Instrumentation; Robotics - Modelling and Control; Feedback Control; Advanced Programming; Machine Learning Concepts and Technologies; Machine Vision & Image Processing.
results.ui_answer: Year 2 lists: Linear Systems & Signals; Electromagnetics with Applications; Electrical Machines and Power Systems; Robotics - Machine Design and Mechanisms; Introduction to Artificial Intelligence; Actuators and Sensors; Electronics Instrumentation; Robotics - Modelling and Control; Feedback Control; Advanced Programming; Machine Learning Concepts and Technologies; Machine Vision & Image Processing.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARA6113 Introduction to Artificial Intelligence.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARA6113 Title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: ARC6113 Computer and Programming Objective: To acquire the knowledge in fundamental principles and techniques of artificial intelligence and skills in building basic artificial intelligence applications. Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Assessment: 10% - Final exam: 50% Laboratory: - Lab 1 – Problem solving with searching - Lab 2 – Machine Learning Course Contents: - Introduction - Problem solving by searching - Evolutionary algorithm and swarm intelligence - Machine learning Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain basic principles and techniques of artificial intelligence. - Solve problems with artificial intelligence techniques. - Build systems that act intelligently and learn from experience. - Demonstrate responsibility in delivering artificial intelligence solutions, recognizing norms and values. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite overridden by programme structure.
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: Computer and Programming Objective: To acquire the knowledge in fundamental principles and techniques of artificial intelligence and skills in building basic artificial intelligence applications. Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Assessment: 10% - Final exam: 50% Laboratory: - Lab 1 – Problem solving with searching - Lab 2 – Machine Learning Course Contents: - Introduction - Problem solving by searching - Evolutionary algorithm and swarm intelligence - Machine learning Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain basic principles and techniques of artificial intelligence. - Solve problems with artificial intelligence techniques. - Build systems that act intelligently and learn from experience. - Demonstrate responsibility in delivering artificial intelligence solutions, recognizing norms and values. results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARA6113 Title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: ARC6113 Computer and Programming Objective: To acquire the knowledge in fundamental principles and techniques of artificial intelligence and skills in building basic artificial intelligence applications. Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Assessment: 10% - Final exam: 50% Laboratory: - Lab 1 – Problem solving with searching - Lab 2 – Machine Learning Course Contents: - Introduction - Problem solving by searching - Evolutionary algorithm and swarm intelligence - Machine learning Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain basic principles and techniques of artificial intelligence. - Solve problems with artificial intelligence techniques. - Build systems that act intelligently and learn from experience. - Demonstrate responsibility in delivering artificial intelligence solutions, recognizing norms and values. results.question: What would I study during Year 2 of Intelligent Robotics?
results.expected_answer: Year 2 lists: Linear Systems & Signals; Electromagnetics with Applications; Electrical Machines and Power Systems; Robotics - Machine Design and Mechanisms; Introduction to Artificial Intelligence; Actuators and Sensors; Electronics Instrumentation; Robotics - Modelling and Control; Feedback Control; Advanced Programming; Machine Learning Concepts and Technologies; Machine Vision & Image Processing.
results.ui_answer: Year 2 for Intelligent Robotics includes Linear Systems & Signals, Electromagnetics with Applications, Electrical Machines and Power Systems, Robotics - Machine Design and Mechanisms, Introduction to Artificial Intelligence, Actuators and Sensors, Electronics Instrumentation, Robotics - Modelling and Control, Feedback Control, Advanced Programming, Machine Learning Concepts and Technologies, Machine Vision & Image Processing.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARL6134 Electromagnetics with Applications.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARL6134 Title: Electromagnetics with Applications Aliases: Electromagnetics With Applications Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2027 Credit Hours: 4 Contact Hours: 59 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide an introduction to electromagnetic theory and its applications related to electronics, electrical machine and telecommunication. Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 30% - Lab Assessment: 10% - Final exam: 40% Course Contents: - Electrostatics and electric currents - Magnetostatics - Electromagnetic induction - Electromagnetic wave - Antennas Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply basic theories of electrostatics and electric currents. - Apply magnetostatics principles to electrical machines. - Build an application utilizing electrostatics and electromagnetic induction. - Demonstrate the characteristics of electromagnetic wave propagation in isotropic mediums. - Evaluate the practical antenna parameters. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
